metadata.title:
THE LAND OF THE


GREAT OKANOCAN

pages.raw_text:
Of this region, which only needs
transportation facilities to fan it to
activity. Great mountains of cop
per, rich claims of silver and gold,
and other minerals lie in the region
of Conconully, Loomis and other
points. Here is the heart of a min,
eral belt, with established values.
which awaits but the magic touch
of the electric transportation to
make 1t known the world over.


Just as this region, once the
favorite haunt of the Indian be-
cause of it's mild winters and early
springs which gave to his ponies a
luXuriant supply of grass and water,
slowly gave way to the great herds
of cattle which followed the advent
of the first hardy pioneers; so now
the onward march of empire. back
ed by the rugged hand of the tiller
of the soil, is changing the face of
nature and driving him, too, to,
gether with his herds, toward a
narrowing range, which will eventu.
ally disappear; while in it's place
will come the wealth of orchard
and the ripened grain
